title: Water Federalism
lead: The division and coordination of powers between Centre and States for ==water governance==. Despite same-party governments, the ==Mahanadi dispute has been pending in its Tribunal since 2018==.

items.title: Constitutional Provisions
bullets: ==Entry 17, State List==: water supplies, irrigation, canals — primarily state subjects
==Entry 56, Union List==: Centre may regulate ==inter-state rivers== if Parliament declares it expedient in the public interest
==Art 262==: Parliament may provide for adjudication of inter-state water disputes AND ==bar SC/court jurisdiction==
Arts 252, 253: consent-based and treaty-implementing legislation

items.title: Key Legislation
bullets: ==Inter-State River Water Disputes Act, 1956==: tribunals; bars court jurisdiction
==River Boards Act, 1956==: never effectively used
Environment (Protection) Act 1986; Water Act 1974 — water quality regulation

items.title: Structural & Legal
bullets: ==Federal-jurisdictional ambiguity== between Centre and States
==Historico-geographical ambiguity==: boundaries changed, agreements outdated
==Institutional ambiguity==: tribunal vs SC roles overlap

items.title: Operational & Political
bullets: ==Decades-long delays== and weak implementation
==Non-compliance== by states citing electoral pressure
==Data deficits== undermine trust
Politicisation — disputes as ==regional-identity tools==

items.title: Technical & Ecological
bullets: ==Supply-augmentation bias== — dams over demand management
==Climate change== breaks old allocation models

head: Practice
Where
What
rows: Basin-level management integrating ecological, social, economic objectives.

items.title: Institutional & Legal
bullets: ==Federal consensus== — water security as national agenda; empower the ==Inter-State Council==
==Redefine the Centre's role== under Entry 56 — facilitator, not just adjudicator
==Robust basin authorities== with genuine stakeholder participation
==Autonomous data institutions== with transparent protocols

items.title: Policy & Fiscal
bullets: ==Performance-based fiscal transfers== incentivising reform
Shift from supply augmentation to ==demand management== and ecosystem restoration
Align ==water–agriculture–energy policies== (MSP, power subsidies) to break perverse incentives

items.title: Participatory & Political
bullets: Multidisciplinary decision bodies; public participation and transparency
==Depoliticise disputes== — discourse aimed at resolution, not identity
Confidence-building for durable agreements
